mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower earnings growth and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high inflation readings and elevated policy rates create stagflationary conditions that erode real corporate margins and suppress valuation multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series are elevated, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a positive coefficient with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery regime, month-over-month increases in industrial production indicate supply chain normalization and capacity utilization improvement, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rising industrial production consistently coincides with flat or negative S&P 500 5-day forward returns, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts dropping below the 1.2 million unit threshold reflects a sharp contraction in business and residential investment, signaling weakening aggregate demand that will depress corporate earnings and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive or neutral when housing starts breach the 1.2M threshold, or the signal exhibits no incremental predictive power over a baseline market model.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strong nonfarm payroll growth and declining inflation readings confirm a soft-landing scenario, reducing recession fears and boosting equity risk appetite, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combination of rising payrolls and falling CPI fails to generate positive forward returns, or the interaction effect reverses sign during periods of elevated market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth consistently exceeding the 2.0% annualized trend line sustains top-line revenue expansion for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically supporting positive forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth prints consistently coincide with negative S&P 500 forward returns, or the signal's information coefficient falls below 0.02 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Within a high-rate tightening regime, any month-over-month increase in the CPI index triggers expectations of further policy tightening, causing immediate repricing of duration risk and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPI upticks fail to produce negative equity returns when policy rates exceed 4.0%, or the market demonstrates complete insulation from inflation-driven rate hike expectations.
